session_handoff.goal: Build a functional, tag-based baseline of the deck (weighted, by 3 modes) to decide which 3 cards to cut to fit Corpse Explosion + Tragic Slip + Victimize, keeping the deck at 100.
session_handoff.method_warning: Scryfall API otag search is UNRELIABLE here — it silently misses real tags (e.g. said Kardur has no goad/drain; called 6 tagged cards 'untagged'). The earlier auto-generated counts table (ramp 24, removal 13, etc.) is RETRACTED. Tag from card text + user rulings; the tagger WEBSITE is the source of truth for raw slugs.

framework_locked.weights: default 1.0; conditional => partial; flex = weight_type splitting modal/MDFC credit across modes (no separate flex tag).

session_handoff.not_started: Auto-tag the ~49 unambiguous cards (mana dorks->ramp, vanilla removal->removal, reanimation spells->haymaker/recursion, lands as mana base). THEN compute the weighted 3-mode stats.

decisions.World Shaper: CUT -> replaced by Overlord of the Hauntwoods (confirmed ramp upgrade)

session_handoff.next_step: Pick up at: (a) greenlight auto-tagging the unambiguous cards + finish Batch 2 with user -> compute weighted 3-mode stats; then (b) use the stats to lock the 3 cuts for Corpse Explosion / Tragic Slip / Victimize. Reminder: deck is at a legal 100 in decklists/henzie_current.txt; friend's print list is decklists/henzie_friend_print.txt (47 cards).

_note: effective_tags = otags from abilities where use=true, after collapse rules. Adjudicated cards below; remaining multi-ability cards pending review.
weight_model: Each effective tag has weight 1.0 (full credit) unless listed in 'weights'. Conditional tags get partial credit (e.g. 0.8 = 'a couple points off' a 10-pt scale). FLEX weight (weight_type=flex): modal/MDFC cards split credit across the modes they serve (e.g. Atsushi ramp 0.5 / card-advantage 0.5) instead of carrying a separate 'flex' tag. CADENCE multiplier (card-level, scales ALL the card's tags): at-will/flexible=1.0, once-per-turn=0.7, once-per-turn+restricted-timing(end-step/sorcery)=0.6. Distinguishes flexible outlets (Greater Good 1.0) from once/turn engines (Industrial Advancement, Birthing Ritual 0.6). Category totals = weighted sums.

abilities.text: When this enters, you may sacrifice another creature. If you do, create Treasures equal to its power.
{2}{B}, Sacrifice X artifacts: Return target creature with power X or less from your graveyard to the battlefield.
abilities.ignore_reason: Treasures always spent on ramp; reanimation mode never used in practice.

abilities.text: Each player exiles all creatures from graveyards, sacrifices all creatures they control, then puts the exiled ones onto the battlefield.
abilities.drop_reason: Cast as a reanimation haymaker, not a wipe; board-clear is incidental.

cards.name: Etali, Primal Storm

abilities.text: Whenever Etali attacks, exile the top card of each player's library, then cast any number of them for free.

cards.user_note: Microfinisher: fat threat cheated down early for political protection (deters/redirects attacks). Generates card + tempo advantage. NOT a table-ending finisher.

cards.name: Kessig Cagebreakers

abilities.text: Whenever this creature attacks, create a 2/2 green Wolf token tapped and attacking for each creature card in your graveyard.

cards.user_note: Genuine finisher: converts the stuffed graveyard into a lethal go-wide attack. Personal-touch card.

cards.name: Champion of Rhonas

abilities.text: You may exert as it attacks. When you do, put a creature card from your hand onto the battlefield.

conditional.deterrent: Needs a worthy creature in hand to be threatening; 6/6 body still blocks.

cards.user_note: Cheats a fat creature from hand into play on attack. Blitz body, dies end of turn, so 'won't untap' is moot.

cards.name: Kardur, Doomscourge

abilities.text: ETB: until your next turn, opponents' creatures attack each combat if able and attack a player other than you if able.
Whenever an attacking creature dies, each opponent loses 1 life and you gain 1.
abilities.drop_reason: Used purely as a deterrent; drain is incidental gravy.

cards.incidental: drain (1 per attacker death) — not counted
cards.user_note: Purely a deterrent: forces the table to swing at each other, not me.

cards.name: Prowling Geistcatcher

abilities.text: Whenever you sacrifice another creature, exile it (token -> +1/+1 on this).
When this leaves the battlefield, return each card exiled with it to the battlefield under your control.

cards.user_note: Sac payoff that banks sacrificed creatures, then dumps them all back when it leaves (e.g. its own blitz death) — one-card board rebuild.

cards.name: Bloodsoaked Insight // Sanguine Morass

abilities.text: Costs {1} less per 1 life opponents lost this turn. Opponent exiles top 3; you may play them until end of your next turn (any mana).
Sanguine Morass: enters tapped, {T}: add B or R.

conditional.card-advantage: Played as a land most of the time; front cast rarely.

cards.user_note: Primarily a B/R land; flips to theft/impulse card-advantage occasionally.

abilities.text: ETB if cast: each player sacrifices all other creatures, then returns all creatures from graveyards (not put there this way) to the battlefield.
abilities.drop_reason: Haymaker reanimation; board-clear incidental (Living Death precedent).

abilities.text: Put all creature cards from all graveyards onto the battlefield under your control.
ETB: fetch a basic land tapped.
Death: draw a card.
ETB: sac another creature, gain X life and draw X (X = power).
Sac a creature: draw cards equal to its power, then discard 3.

cards.name: Atsushi, the Blazing Sky

abilities.text: Death, choose one: impulse top 2 cards (card-advantage) OR create 3 Treasures (ramp).

cards.weight_status: proposed 0.5/0.5 — pending your split (treasures vs impulse)

cards.name: Overlord of the Hauntwoods

abilities.text: Whenever this permanent enters or attacks, create a tapped colorless land token named Everywhere that is every basic land type.

cards.user_note: Confirmed replacement for World Shaper (CUT). Impending 4-{1}{G}{G} lets it land early as a non-creature enchantment; ramps a land on ETB and again on every attack once it's a 6/5 creature, vs. World Shaper's one-shot death-trigger land recursion.

abilities.text: Whenever you sacrifice a creature, may return a lesser-MV creature from grave tapped. Once each turn.

cards.name: Industrial Advancement

abilities.text: End step: may sac a creature, dig X (=its MV), put a creature into play.

cards.user_note: Blitz synergy: converts an end-of-turn-dying creature into a cheated-in creature.

abilities.text: End step: dig 7, may sac a creature, put a creature MV <= (sacMV+1) into play.

category_defs.deterrent: Political/defensive: being a scary board presence (or redirecting attacks, e.g. goad) to deter aggression at you. DISTINCT from otag 'protection' (mechanically shielding a creature from removal).
category_defs.haymaker: Big swingy play that lurches the game in your favor (mass board rebuild, mass reanimation). High-impact but not necessarily lethal — Mode 3. SYNONYM: mass-reanimation folds into this.
category_defs.finisher: Closes the game / win condition (e.g. Kessig Cagebreakers turning the graveyard into a lethal swing).
category_defs._flex_DEPRECATED: Retired as a category. Flexibility is now expressed via weight_type=flex (split partial weights across modes) — see weight_model.
